summary: M.S. Data Science researcher working on geospatial AI and robust spatiotemporal vision. Current focus: benchmark validity, geographic transfer, and uncertainty under shift in Earth-observation ML.
research_interests: My research is on evaluation methodology and out-of-distribution generalization for spatiotemporal vision. I work on input-design covariates that confound architectural comparisons in earth-observation models (channel count, temporal horizon, geographic distribution), and on whether observed differences hold up under sealed holdouts and zero-shot geographic transfer. Broader interests: robust computer vision, distribution-shift evaluation, calibrated uncertainty in deep learning.

education.thesis: Multi-Horizon Urban Growth Prediction: A Comparative Deep Learning Study
education.focus: Distribution shift, evaluation methodology, and uncertainty quantification in spatiotemporal vision

education.degree: B.Tech in Computer and Communication Engineering
education.department: School of Computing and Information Technology

publications.venue: GeoAI 2026, Ghent, Belgium (Oral). Also at SpatialDI 2026, Springer LNCS proceedings, ACM SIGSPATIAL China Chapter, Changsha, China.

in_preparation.title: Train Anywhere, Test Everywhere: Cross-Region Transfer in Earth Observation Is Decided by the Data, Not the Model
in_preparation.note: Sole author. Prepared as a SIGSPATIAL '26 short paper; follow-on to the channel-count audit. The full source-by-target transfer matrix over twenty world regions shows the training source is inert: the target sets the score (home-field advantage -0.001 FoM, source rows at Spearman 0.97, ordering identical across six architectures incl. fine-tuned foundation models), and a parameter-free recent-past baseline tops every trained model (FoM 0.56 vs 0.34, IoU 0.92 vs 0.61). Swapping the input traces a provenance spectrum, from harmonised products that transfer to raw sensor reflectance that does not; the same target-over-model dominance recurs on the PANGAEA benchmark. Code at github.com/rohanbalixz/Cross-Region-Source-Invariance-in-Earth-Observation.

in_preparation.title: UrbanFinance: Forecasting the Latent Land-Demand Residual at 100 m across Divergent Indian Urban Typologies
in_preparation.note: Sole author. Predicts the signed gap between economically expressed demand and EO-realized supply across five divergent Indian metros, fusing free GHSL, night-lights, terrain, and NHB RESIDEX layers. The DSRF model beats SimVP, TAU, and PredRNN.V2 on Figure of Merit at p<0.05 over ten seeds, gives calibrated 95% prediction intervals, and under leave-one-city-out transfers to an unseen city better than SOTA trained on all five.

research_projects.title: UrbanFinance: Latent Land-Demand Forecasting across Indian Metros
research_projects.description: Forecasts the latent land-demand residual at 100 m across five divergent Indian urban typologies (Noida, Mumbai, Chandigarh, Bengaluru, Hyderabad), fusing free Earth observation with guideline-value and transaction records.
highlights: DSRF beats SimVP, TAU, and PredRNN.V2 on Figure of Merit at p<0.05 over 10 seeds, with calibrated 95% prediction intervals
Leave-one-city-out transfer to an unseen metro beats SOTA trained on all five cities
Saiz-style supply economics: constrained cities convert less land (corr -0.77) and carry more suppressed demand (corr +0.79)

research_projects.title: Multi-Horizon Urban-Growth Prediction (Channel-Count Confound)
research_projects.description: Continental-scale benchmark audit of CNN and ConvLSTM comparisons on multi-horizon urban-growth prediction using GHSL R2023A over CONUS.
highlights: Sealed 2020 temporal holdout; channel-matched horizon controls
MC Dropout calibration r = 0.983 across 8.69M validation pixels
SpatialDI 2026 (Springer LNCS) main paper. GeoAI 2026 short paper (oral), published on Zenodo. SpatialDI PDF forthcoming.

research_projects.title: Learning from Minimal Data under Distribution Shift (Master's thesis)
research_projects.description: Frameworks for data-efficient learning when supervision is scarce and the distribution moves; the boundary between representation learning, robustness, and evaluation.
highlights: Investigating robustness and adaptation in low-data regimes
Empirical study of representation-learning trade-offs under shift

research_projects.title: Disaster Risk Monitoring from Satellite Radar (NVIDIA DLI)
research_projects.description: U-Net flood segmentation on Sentinel-1 SAR with preprocessing tuned to radar noise.
highlights: Dice = 0.82, IoU = 0.78 on UNOSAT flood event
Earth Engine integration for real-time risk assessment
